message:
fix(demo): detect public host to force demo mode on brennerbot.org

The sessions list page was failing on brennerbot.org because BRENNER_LAB_MODE
is enabled on Vercel for internal features, but the public site should always
show demo mode regardless of that setting.

This fix adds hostname detection to force demo mode when the request comes
from brennerbot.org, ensuring public visitors see the demo sessions list.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -578,7 +578,7 @@ {"id":"brenner_bot-m6yw","title":"Feature: Test→Hypothesis Binding","description":"# Feature: Test→Hypothesis Binding (ENHANCED) ## Purpose Close the research loop by formally connecting test execution to hypothesis state changes. **CRITICAL UPDATES**: Predictions are now first-class entities, and tests MUST have potency checks. ## CRITICAL UPDATES from Review After reviewing the artifact schema and evaluation rubric: ### Issue 1: Predictions Must Be First-Class The artifact schema separates **Predictions Table (Section 3)** from **Discriminative Tests (Section 4)**: - A **Prediction** is: "Under condition X, H1 predicts Y, H2 predicts Z" - A **Test** is: "Do X and observe the result" These are DISTINCT because: - Multiple tests might address the same prediction - A prediction might be tested multiple times with different methods ### Issue 2: Potency Checks Are MANDATORY From evaluation rubric "Potency Check Sufficiency" (0-3): - 0: No potency check included → **AUTOMATIC FAILURE** - 3: Complete potency check: positive control, sensitivity verification, timing validation From artifact schema: "Each test MUST include a potency check" ### Issue 3: Tests Need Evidence-Per-Week Scoring The artifact schema requires tests to have a 12-point score across 4 dimensions: - Likelihood ratio (0-3): How discriminative? - Cost (0-3): How expensive? - Speed (0-3): How fast? - Ambiguity (0-3): How clear is the readout? ### Issue 4: Object Transposition From evaluation rubric "Object Transposition Considered" (0-2): - Has the contributor considered alternative experimental systems? ## Enhanced Schemas ### Prediction Schema (NEW - First-Class Entity) ```typescript export const PredictionSchema = z.object({ /** Stable ID: P-{session_id}-{sequence} */ id: z.string().regex(/^P-[A-Z0-9]+-\d{3}$/), /** The observable condition */ condition: z.string().min(10), /** What each hypothesis predicts for this condition */ hypothesisPredictions: z.array(z.object({ hypothesisId: z.string(), prediction: z.string(), rationale: z.string().optional(), })).min(2), // Must discriminate at least 2 hypotheses /** Session where prediction was formulated */ sessionId: z.string(), /** Which tests address this prediction? */ linkedTests: z.array(z.string()).optional(), // T-xxx IDs createdAt: z.string().datetime(), }); ``` ### Enhanced Test Schema ```typescript export const PotencyCheckSchema = z.object({ /** Positive control that would show the effect if present */ positiveControl: z.string(), /** How do we verify detection threshold is adequate? */ sensitivityVerification: z.string().optional(), /** How do we know the assay window is correct? */ timingValidation: z.string().optional(), }); export const EvidencePerWeekScoreSchema = z.object({ likelihoodRatio: z.number().min(0).max(3), // 0=<2:1, 1=2-10:1, 2=10-100:1, 3=>100:1 cost: z.number().min(0).max(3), // 0=>$100K, 1=$10K-$100K, 2=$1K-$10K, 3=<$1K speed: z.number().min(0).max(3), // 0=>1yr, 1=1-6mo, 2=1wk-1mo, 3=<1wk ambiguity: z.number().min(0).max(3), // 0=many confounds, 3=digital readout total: z.number().min(0).max(12), }); export const TestRecordSchema = z.object({ /** Stable ID: T-{session_id}-{sequence} */ id: z.string().regex(/^T-[A-Z0-9]+-\d{3}$/), /** Test name for display */ name: z.string(), /** Full procedure description */ procedure: z.string().min(20), /** Which hypotheses does this test discriminate? */ discriminates: z.array(z.string()).min(2), // H-xxx IDs /** Expected outcomes per hypothesis */ expectedOutcomes: z.array(z.object({ hypothesisId: z.string(), outcome: z.string(), })), /** Which predictions does this test address? */ addressesPredictions: z.array(z.string()).optional(), // P-xxx IDs /** MANDATORY: Potency check */ potencyCheck: PotencyCheckSchema, // NOT optional! /** 4-dimension scoring */ evidencePerWeekScore: EvidencePerWeekScoreSchema, /** Object transposition notes (alternative experimental systems) */ objectTransposition: z.object({ considered: z.boolean(), alternatives: z.array(z.object({ system: z.string(), pros: z.string(), cons: z.string(), })).optional(), chosenRationale: z.string().optional(), }).optional(), /** Feasibility assessment */ feasibility: z.object({ requirements: z.string(), difficulty: z.enum(['easy', 'moderate', 'hard', 'very_hard']), blockers: z.array(z.string()).optional(), }), /** Which assumptions does this test require? */ requiredAssumptions: z.array(z.string()).optional(), // A-xxx IDs /** Session where test was designed */ designedInSession: z.string(), /** Test status */ status: z.enum(['designed', 'in_progress', 'completed', 'blocked']), /** If completed, execution record */ execution: TestExecutionSchema.optional(), createdAt: z.string().datetime(), updatedAt: z.string().datetime(), }); ``` ## Validation Rules From evaluation rubric: **Discriminative Power (0-3):** - Test MUST have different expected outcomes for each hypothesis - Outcomes should be binary or quantitatively large differences - Both outcomes must be actually observable **Potency Check Sufficiency (0-3):** - AUTOMATIC FAILURE if no potency check - Must distinguish "no effect" from "assay failed" **Score Calibration Honesty (0-2):** - Scores should be conservative, not all 3s ## CLI Commands ### Prediction Commands (NEW) - `brenner prediction list` - `brenner prediction show ` - `brenner prediction create --condition="..." --h1-predicts="..." --h2-predicts="..."` ### Test Commands (Enhanced) - `brenner test create` — Now REQUIRES potency check, scores - `brenner test validate ` — Check test meets all requirements - `brenner test execute ` — Record execution (unchanged) ## Success Criteria - [ ] Predictions are first-class entities with own schema/storage - [ ] Tests MUST have potency checks (validation error if missing) - [ ] Tests have 4-dimension evidence-per-week scores - [ ] Object transposition tracking available - [ ] Test validation warns on inflated scores - [ ] Links between predictions, tests, and hypotheses ## Related Specs - specs/artifact_schema_v0.1.md — Sections 3, 4 - specs/evaluation_rubric_v0.1.md — Criteria 7, 8, 9, Fixed validation bugs in createBinaryPrediction and createBinaryTest.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-m6yw","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-lm3b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:42:04.124513825-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-m6yw","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-6n4e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:42:04.155992056-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-m6yw","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-jcuz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:37:47.395000148-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-mat6","title":"TASK: Create Entry Points from Existing Site to Brenner Loop","description":" ## How Do Users Discover the Brenner Loop? The Brenner Loop is the crown jewel of brennerbot.org, but users need to find it! Currently, the site has corpus browsing and distillations. We need clear pathways to the interactive experience. ### Entry Point Locations #### 1. Landing Page CTA Add a prominent section on the landing page: \`\`\` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ 🔬 Ready to Apply the Method? │ │ │ │ Start an interactive research session and learn to │ │ think like Sydney Brenner. Our AI-powered tools help │ │ you develop and test discriminative hypotheses. │ │ │ │ [Start Your First Session] [Watch Tutorial] │ │ │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ \`\`\` #### 2. Header Navigation Add to main navigation: - "Research Lab" or "Start Session" as top-level nav item - Dropdown with: My Sessions, New Session, Tutorial #### 3. Operator Pages Integration Each operator page (Level Split, Exclusion Test, etc.) should have: - "Try it in a Session" button - Opens /sessions/new with that operator pre-selected #### 4. After Tutorial Completion Tutorial completion screen should prominently feature: - "Apply what you learned" - Direct link to start a new session #### 5. Corpus Deep Links When reading corpus content, contextual links like: - "Brenner discusses X — explore this in a session" - "Apply Level Split to your own research" ### First-Time User Flow \`\`\` User lands on brennerbot.org │ ▼ Sees "Research Lab" in nav OR landing CTA │ ▼ /sessions → Empty state with: - "Welcome! Start your first session" - Link to tutorial - "Or jump right in" │ ▼ /sessions/new → Guided intake \`\`\` ### Empty States When /sessions is empty: \`\`\` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ 🎯 No Sessions Yet │ │ │ │ A Brenner Loop session helps you rigorously test │ │ hypotheses using discriminative methodology. │ │ │ │ New to this? [Start with the Tutorial] │ │ │ │ Ready to go? [Create Your First Session] │ │ │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ \`\`\` ### File Locations - Update: apps/web/src/app/page.tsx (landing) - Update: apps/web/src/components/layout/header.tsx - Update: apps/web/src/app/operators/[slug]/page.tsx - Update: tutorial completion component ### Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Landing page has clear CTA for sessions - [ ] Header navigation includes sessions link - [ ] Empty state guides first-time users - [ ] Operator pages link to sessions - [ ] Tutorial completion links to sessions ### Depends On - Routing structure task ","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T03:26:25.567524128-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:15:15.536158178-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T15:15:15.536158178-05:00","close_reason":"Completed entry points implementation","labels":["brenner-loop","landing","navigation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-mat6","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-pts6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T03:26:53.591364921-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-mhi","title":"TanStack Store: Create Compare View State Persistence","description":"# Goal Persist the distillation comparison view state so users don't lose their selections. ## Background The CompareView component has UI state that should persist: - leftModel: Which model is in left pane (opus/gpt/gemini) - rightModel: Which model is in right pane - syncScroll: Whether panes scroll together - Per-model scroll positions Currently, refreshing the page resets all this state. ## Store Schema ```typescript interface CompareStore { leftModel: 'opus' | 'gpt' | 'gemini'; rightModel: 'opus' | 'gpt' | 'gemini'; syncScroll: boolean; scrollPositions: Record; // model -> scrollY } ``` ## Implementation ```typescript import { Store } from '@tanstack/store'; const compareStore = new Store({ leftModel: 'opus', rightModel: 'gpt', syncScroll: true, scrollPositions: {}, }); // Persist to localStorage compareStore.subscribe(() => { localStorage.setItem('brenner-compare', JSON.stringify(compareStore.state)); }); // React hook export function useCompareState() { return useStore(compareStore, (s) => s); } export function setLeftModel(model: Model) { compareStore.setState((s) => ({ ...s, leftModel: model })); } ``` ## Integration ```typescript function CompareView() { const { leftModel, rightModel, syncScroll } = useCompareState(); return (
{/* ... */}
); } ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Model selections persist - [ ] Sync scroll preference persists - [ ] Per-model scroll positions persist - [ ] State restores on page load ## Files to Create - apps/web/src/stores/compareStore.ts - apps/web/src/hooks/useCompareState.ts ## Files to Modify - apps/web/src/components/distillation/CompareView.tsx","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:20:13.355992416-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T00:44:46.446051904-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T00:44:46.446051904-05:00","close_reason":"Obsolete: Compare view feature was removed in commit 34a5aa6","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-mhi","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-59l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:20:36.726561983-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]} -{"id":"brenner_bot-mmxk","title":"Random code exploration + fresh-eyes bug hunt (additional modules)","description":"Inspect unrelated modules for latent bugs; fix any found.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T12:36:51.443631241-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T12:37:01.37321942-05:00"} +{"id":"brenner_bot-mmxk","title":"Random code exploration + fresh-eyes bug hunt (additional modules)","description":"Inspect unrelated modules for latent bugs; fix any found.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T12:36:51.443631241-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T12:38:21.466850131-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-06T12:38:21.466850131-05:00","close_reason":"Completed"} {"id":"brenner_bot-momc","title":"Triage coverage gaps and configure per-file thresholds","description":"Before writing tests, configure vitest.config.ts with appropriate per-file thresholds. This alone may fix most CI failures. The config already supports per-file thresholds (see artifact-merge.ts example). Strategy: (1) EXCLUDE static data files like tutorial-data/*.ts and analytics.ts, (2) SET LOWER thresholds for operators (50%), React hooks (30%), state machines (60%), (3) DOCUMENT rationale in code comments. This is the highest leverage task - may fix CI without writing any tests.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-05T12:46:09.288266087-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-05T13:04:27.20854215-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-05T13:04:27.20854215-05:00","close_reason":"Adjusted Vitest coverage gates/excludes to unblock CI (momc)"} {"id":"brenner_bot-mqg7","title":"Feature: Operator Intervention Audit Trail","description":"# Goal Implement a formal audit trail for human operator interventions during Brenner Loop sessions, enabling reproducibility, trust verification, and learning from overrides. ## The Gap This Fills Other agents covered: - Human-in-the-loop gates (general concept) - Cockpit runner (orchestration) - Web UI for sessions **What nobody covered:** When a human operator overrides an agent decision (e.g., manually killing a hypothesis, forcing a test to be included, editing an artifact before publish), there is no formal record of: - WHAT was changed - WHO made the change - WHY they made it (rationale) - WHEN it happened relative to agent activities This matters because: 1. **Reproducibility** - Can someone else replicate this research if they dont know a human secretly tweaked the artifact? 2. **Trust** - Was this conclusion reached by agents following the protocol, or did a human push it through? 3. **Learning** - If operators frequently override a certain agent/role, maybe the role prompt needs improvement. 4. **Debugging** - When a session goes wrong, was it agent error or operator error? ## Why This Is Different from Deltas Agent contributions are recorded as DELTA messages with `from: AgentName`. But operator interventions can happen in several ways that bypass the delta flow: 1. **Direct artifact editing** before publish (editing the markdown) 2. **Selective delta exclusion** (compiling without some deltas) 3. **Manual message injection** (posting a DELTA on behalf of an agent) 4. **Override decisions** ("ignore this critique", "force-include this test") 5. **Session control actions** (early termination, forced re-run, role reassignment) ## Proposed Solution ### 1. Operator Intervention Record ```typescript interface OperatorIntervention { id: string; session_id: string; timestamp: string; operator_id: string; // From BRENNER_AGENT_NAME or "human" type: | "artifact_edit" // Direct edit to compiled artifact | "delta_exclusion" // Excluded a delta from compilation | "delta_injection" // Added a delta not from an agent | "decision_override" // Overrode a protocol decision | "session_control" // Terminated, forked, or reset session | "role_reassignment"; // Changed agent-role mappings mid-session target: { message_id?: number; // For delta exclusion/injection artifact_version?: number; item_id?: string; // Hypothesis, test, etc. }; before?: string; // State before intervention (for diffs) after?: string; // State after intervention rationale: string; // Required: WHY did the operator do this? reversible: boolean; reversed_at?: string; reversed_by?: string; } ``` ### 2. Intervention Capture Points | Action | Capture Point | Implementation | |--------|---------------|----------------| | Edit artifact before publish | Compare pre/post in publish action | API route adds intervention record | | Exclude delta | Compile action with exclusion list | Track excluded message_ids | | Manual delta | DELTA message where from = operator | Check if sender matches known agent roster | | Early termination | Session close action | Add "session_control" record | | Role reassignment | Roster change mid-session | Compare before/after roster state | ### 3. Storage and Access **Storage:** Interventions stored as part of session metadata in Agent Mail thread (as INFO: messages with structured payload) or in a separate interventions log. **Access:** - `brenner session interventions ` - List all interventions - Session detail page shows intervention count and expandable list - Compiled artifacts include intervention summary in metadata ### 4. Intervention Severity Levels ``` MINOR: Typo fixes, formatting adjustments MODERATE: Delta exclusion, small edits MAJOR: Killing hypotheses, adding tests, role changes CRITICAL: Session termination, protocol bypass ``` Major/Critical interventions trigger warnings in the UI. ### 5. Reproducibility Mode A "strict" compilation mode that: - Fails if any MAJOR+ interventions exist - Produces a "clean" artifact that is purely agent-derived - Useful for comparing "what would agents have produced alone?" ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] `OperatorIntervention` type defined in session types - [ ] Publish action detects and records artifact edits - [ ] Compile action accepts `excludeDeltas` param and records exclusions - [ ] Manual delta detection (sender not in agent roster) - [ ] CLI `brenner session interventions ` command - [ ] Web session detail shows intervention badge/list - [ ] All interventions require `rationale` field - [ ] Compiled artifact metadata includes `intervention_count` ## Dependencies - Agent roster (brenner_bot-5so.12.1.x) - needed to detect "operator vs agent" - Session compile/publish actions (already exist) ## Non-Goals - Not preventing interventions (operators should have full control) - Not implementing approval workflows (thats for future governance) - Not tracking every click/action (only meaningful interventions) ## Background: Why This Matters for Science Real science has lab notebooks that record everything, including mistakes and corrections. The audit trail is the digital equivalent. Without it, a "research result" from a Brenner Loop session cannot be trusted or replicated by others. The Brenner Method emphasizes transparency and honesty about what was done and why. Occams Broom (hiding inconvenient facts) is an anti-pattern. The audit trail ensures operator interventions are visible, not swept under the rug.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"GoldFalcon","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:01:31.405372314-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-01T20:48:36.816169242-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-01T20:48:36.816169242-05:00","close_reason":"Schema, storage layer, and artifact metadata complete. Remaining integration work (publish/compile capture, CLI commands) can be follow-up tasks.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-mqg7","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-jcuz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:38:22.90910216-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-mth","title":"Task: Unit tests for lib/parsers.ts","description":"Write comprehensive unit tests for document parsers. ## Functions to Test (need to verify file exists first) - Markdown parsing - Section extraction - Anchor parsing - Metadata extraction ## Test Fixtures - Use real corpus documents as fixtures - Include edge cases: empty, malformed, large files ## Test Cases - Valid markdown parsing - Section boundary detection - Anchor/citation extraction (§n format) - Error handling for invalid input - Performance with large documents","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:10:34.204022025-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T22:56:07.573038512-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T22:56:07.573038512-05:00","close_reason":"Completed: Added 109 comprehensive unit tests for all parsers (transcript, distillation, quotebank, metaprompt)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-mth","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-0b5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:10:57.497567726-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-mth","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-l8y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:10:59.317778324-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-mth","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-hnn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:12:32.676627321-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]} @@ -663,6 +663,7 @@ {"id":"brenner_bot-saw4","title":"Boost branch coverage for near-threshold files","description":"Add targeted tests for files that are close to the 75% branch threshold. These need only 1-3 additional test cases each to pass. Files: sessionHypothesis.ts (70.73%), session-kickoff.ts (70.79%), threadStatus.ts (73.23%), test-storage.ts (73.41%), hypothesis-storage.ts (74.57%), operator-library.ts (66.66%). Focus on untested conditional branches - check coverage report HTML for exact lines. ~10-15 targeted test cases total.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SunnyGrove","created_at":"2026-01-05T12:46:47.474912001-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-05T17:53:44.950462089-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-05T17:53:44.950462089-05:00","close_reason":"All near-threshold files now pass 75% branch coverage: sessionHypothesis.ts, session-kickoff.ts, threadStatus.ts (previous session), test-storage.ts (78.48%), hypothesis-storage.ts (83.05%). Re-enabled in vitest coverage gates. operator-library.ts (66.66%) is separate effort.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-saw4","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-momc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-05T12:46:57.006544936-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-se2r","title":"TASK: Define Hypothesis Lifecycle State Machine","description":" ## Formal States for Hypothesis Evolution Hypotheses aren't just "open" or "closed" - they have a rich lifecycle. This task defines the formal state machine. ### Hypothesis States 1. **Draft** - Initial creation, freely editable - No predictions locked yet - Can be deleted without penalty 2. **Active** - User is actively working on this hypothesis - Has at least one locked prediction - Cannot be deleted (only superseded or falsified) 3. **Testing** - Evidence collection in progress - Predictions locked, awaiting results - Confidence updates happening 4. **Supported** - Evidence consistent with hypothesis - High robustness score (passed tests) - May still be tested further 5. **Falsified** - Clear discriminative evidence against - Moved to Hypothesis Graveyard - Learning captured (what did we learn?) 6. **Superseded** - Replaced by a better formulation - Link to successor hypothesis - Still visible in history 7. **Dormant** - User paused work (no activity for N days) - Can be reactivated - Preserved but not shown in active list ### State Transitions - Draft → Active: First prediction locked - Active → Testing: Evidence collection starts - Testing → Supported: Predictions confirmed - Testing → Falsified: Predictions refuted - Active → Superseded: User creates refined version - Active → Dormant: No activity for 30 days - Dormant → Active: User resumes work ### Implementation - Add `state` field to HypothesisCard - State transition functions with validation - UI indicators for each state (icons, colors) - Filters in session list by state ### Depends On - an1n.1 (HypothesisCard Interface) ","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:51:23.309616577-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T11:43:52.589020594-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T11:43:52.589020594-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented hypothesis lifecycle state machine with 7 states (draft, active, testing, supported, falsified, superseded, dormant), transition guards, UI configuration, and lifecycle statistics","labels":["brenner-loop","hypothesis-engine","state-machine"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-se2r","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-an1n.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:51:47.763351355-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-sedg","title":"FEATURE: Undo/Redo System for Session Actions","description":" ## Research is Iterative Users will make mistakes and want to undo: - Accidentally locked a prediction too early - Recorded wrong evidence observation - Updated confidence incorrectly - Discarded a hypothesis prematurely ### Command Pattern Implementation ```typescript interface SessionCommand { id: string; type: string; timestamp: Date; // For undo execute(): Promise; undo(): Promise; // For display description: string; } class UpdateConfidenceCommand implements SessionCommand { constructor( private hypothesisId: string, private previousConfidence: number, private newConfidence: number, private evidenceId: string ) {} async execute() { await updateHypothesisConfidence(this.hypothesisId, this.newConfidence); } async undo() { await updateHypothesisConfidence(this.hypothesisId, this.previousConfidence); await markEvidenceReverted(this.evidenceId); } get description() { return `Update confidence: ${this.previousConfidence}% → ${this.newConfidence}%`; } } ``` ### Undo Stack Management ```typescript interface UndoManager { history: SessionCommand[]; undoneStack: SessionCommand[]; // For redo maxHistory: number; execute(command: SessionCommand): Promise; undo(): Promise; redo(): Promise; canUndo: boolean; canRedo: boolean; } ``` ### Undoable Actions - Confidence updates - Hypothesis state changes - Evidence recording - Prediction modifications (before lock) - Session phase transitions (with warning) ### Non-Undoable Actions (by design) - Prediction LOCK (intentionally irreversible) - Session export/share - Agent dispatch (can't un-ask an agent) ### UI Integration ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ Session: Social Media Hypothesis │ ├─────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ [↩ Undo] [↪ Redo] History ▾ │ ├─────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ │ │ Recent Actions: │ │ • Updated confidence 72% → 49% [↩] │ │ • Recorded evidence: Algorithm... [↩] │ │ • Applied Level Split operator [↩] │ │ │ └─────────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` ### Keyboard Shortcuts - Ctrl+Z / Cmd+Z: Undo - Ctrl+Shift+Z / Cmd+Shift+Z: Redo ### Persistence Undo history persisted with session (LocalStorage): - Survives page refresh - Cleared on session close ### File Location `apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/undoManager.ts` `apps/web/src/hooks/useUndoRedo.ts` `apps/web/src/components/brenner-loop/UndoRedoControls.tsx` ### Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Core undo/redo works for main actions - [ ] History preserved across refresh - [ ] Keyboard shortcuts work - [ ] Non-undoable actions clearly marked ### Depends On - 1v26.3 (Session Context Provider) - reew.3 (Session State Machine) {"id":"brenner_bot-slcc","title":"Task: Define State Transition Types","description":"# Task: Define State Transition Types ## Objective Define the TypeScript types for state transitions between hypothesis lifecycle states. ## Implementation Details ### File Location `apps/web/src/lib/hypothesis-transitions.ts` ### Type Definitions ```typescript import { z } from 'zod'; import { HypothesisState, HypothesisStateSchema } from './hypothesis'; export const TransitionTriggerSchema = z.enum([ 'activate', // proposed → active 'refine', // active → refined 'kill', // active → killed 'validate', // active → validated 'park', // proposed|active → dormant 'reactivate', // dormant → active ]); export type TransitionTrigger = z.infer; export const StateTransitionSchema = z.object({ hypothesisId: z.string(), fromState: HypothesisStateSchema, toState: HypothesisStateSchema, trigger: TransitionTriggerSchema, triggeredBy: z.string().optional(), // Test ID, agent name, or 'user' reason: z.string().optional(), metadata: z.record(z.unknown()).optional(), // Additional context timestamp: z.string().datetime(), }); export type StateTransition = z.infer; ``` ### Transition Matrix ```typescript /** Valid state transitions: [fromState] → [toState[]] */ export const VALID_TRANSITIONS: Record = { proposed: ['active', 'dormant'], active: ['refined', 'killed', 'validated', 'dormant'], dormant: ['active'], refined: [], // Terminal killed: [], // Terminal validated: ['refined', 'killed'], // Can be re-evaluated }; /** Which trigger causes which transition */ export const TRIGGER_TRANSITIONS: Record = { activate: { from: ['proposed', 'dormant'], to: 'active' }, refine: { from: ['active', 'validated'], to: 'refined' }, kill: { from: ['active', 'validated'], to: 'killed' }, validate: { from: ['active'], to: 'validated' }, park: { from: ['proposed', 'active'], to: 'dormant' }, reactivate: { from: ['dormant'], to: 'active' }, }; ``` ### Validation Function ```typescript export function isValidTransition( fromState: HypothesisState, toState: HypothesisState ): boolean { return VALID_TRANSITIONS[fromState]?.includes(toState) ?? false; } export function getRequiredTrigger( fromState: HypothesisState, toState: HypothesisState ): TransitionTrigger | null { for (const [trigger, config] of Object.entries(TRIGGER_TRANSITIONS)) { if (config.from.includes(fromState) && config.to === toState) { return trigger as TransitionTrigger; } } return null; } ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All transition types defined with Zod - [ ] Transition matrix covers all valid paths - [ ] Validation function correctly rejects invalid transitions - [ ] Terminal states (killed, refined) cannot transition out - [ ] Clear error messages for invalid transitions ## Dependencies - Feature: Hypothesis Registry Schema (for HypothesisState type) ## Effort Estimate ~100 lines of code, ~1 Quality gates: bun test brenner.test.ts + bun run test (Home/Corpus/Method page tests) + ubs --diff clean.","created_at":"2025-12-30T15:38:51Z"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-t62l","title":"Spec: Experiment result capture protocol (wrapper-run + record)","description":"# Goal Define and implement how to **capture experiment runs** so they become durable, auditable inputs to Brenner Loop sessions. This is the bridge between: - “artifact contains discriminative tests (T#)” - and “a test run produced evidence we can cite and update against” --- # v0 stance - Capture is about **what happened**, not what it means. - Execution is explicit operator action (no auto-running code from agent messages). - v0 is **wrapper-run + capture**, not a hardened sandbox. --- # Filesystem discipline (recommended) Keep a clean separation between source and outputs: - Source/workspace: the repo/folder you are studying (Agent Mail `project_key`) - Results: session-scoped directory under `artifacts/` Suggested layout: ``` / artifacts/ / results/ T1_.json ... optional generated artifacts ... ``` --- # Result schema (draft) ```ts interface ExperimentResult { // Identity result_id: string; thread_id: string; test_id: string; // Execution command: string; cwd: string; started_at: string; // ISO duration_ms: number; timeout_seconds?: number; // Outcome exit_code: number; stdout: string; stderr: string; // Optional provenance git_sha?: string; git_dirty?: boolean; files_changed?: string[]; } ``` --- # CLI commands (v0) ```bash # Wrapper-run (recommended) ./brenner.ts experiment run \ --thread-id RS-... \ --test-id T1 \ --timeout 300 \ -- python test_rrp.py --variant rrp # Manual record (when run elsewhere) ./brenner.ts experiment record \ --thread-id RS-... \ --test-id T1 \ --exit-code 0 \ --stdout-file out.txt \ --stderr-file err.txt ``` --- # Safety guidance (documented, not enforced) - Only run commands you trust. - Always use `--timeout`. - Treat stdout/stderr as potentially containing secrets. - Prefer running from a clean git state; record `git_sha` + dirty state. --- # Acceptance criteria - [ ] `ExperimentResult` contract is locked (schema + fixtures) - [ ] `experiment run` captures stdout/stderr/exit code/duration + provenance - [ ] `experiment record` exists for manual entry - [ ] Result files are session-scoped and easy to locate ","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-30T18:50:52.403902581-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T23:50:15.656443873-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T23:50:15.656443873-05:00","close_reason":"Spec complete at specs/experiment_capture_protocol_v0.1.md. Implementation verified in brenner.ts with experiment run/record commands and 12 passing tests.","labels":["infrastructure","research-loop","safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-t62l","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.12","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T18:59:49.820667903-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
